title: PR #48 review: gate restore_current_handler on fresh-backup flag
description: Codex review (https://github.com/elviskahoro/gtm-sdk/pull/48#discussion_r3257118674) noted that the EXIT trap installed in scripts/deploy_webhook.sh at the lock-acquisition block can restore a STALE backup from tmp/webhook-deploy-bak/ if the current invocation fails between trap install and the fresh backup write (e.g. during Modal/GCS preflight). Gate restore_current_handler on a BACKUP_FRESHLY_WRITTEN flag so the trap is a no-op until this invocation has actually written the backup.
close_reason: Gated restore_current_handler on BACKUP_FRESHLY_WRITTEN flag. Trap is now a no-op until this invocation has actually written its own backup, so a stale backup from a prior run cannot be restored on top of the current HEAD.

title: Harden webhook deploy story
description: Umbrella for follow-up work after agent/fix-deploy-webhook-review. Covers verification (CI), documentation, and ongoing operator ergonomics for scripts/deploy_webhook.sh.

title: Add CI smoke test for scripts/deploy_webhook.sh
description:
Why: agent/fix-deploy-webhook-review just fixed several gotchas (MODAL_TOKEN_ID env clobber, cp -i alias, infisical inline-variable expansion, missing trap on EXIT). Without a smoke test these will silently regress on the next refactor.

Acceptance: a CI job that exercises the deploy loop's substitution + restore logic against a stub modal binary, asserting the working tree is restored even on mid-loop failure.
acceptance_criteria:
- CI job runs deploy_webhook.sh against a stub modal binary
- Working tree is restored even when the loop is killed mid-iteration (EXIT trap fires)
- MODAL_TOKEN_ID/SECRET injected by infisical wins over any parent-shell values
- cp_-i alias does not silently answer 'no' to the placeholder restore

title: Document scripts/deploy_webhook.sh in webhooks/README.md
description:
Why: the script has 5+ non-obvious gotchas (documented in gtm-sdk/CLAUDE.md 'Scripted deploy pitfalls'). A README next to the webhooks themselves is where an operator will actually look. Should follow the smoke test so docs reflect verified behavior, not aspiration.

Acceptance: webhooks/README.md exists, covers per-source deploy command, the WebhookModelToReplace placeholder, and the env-clobber/cp-alias/infisical-expansion pitfalls.
